channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: McConnell 'fine' after stumbling on Senate steps, using wheelchair as precaution
item.description: (AP) -- Sen. Mitch McConnell fell slightly on a few steps while exiting the Senate chamber Wednesday after late-morning votes. The Republican former leader was immediately scooped up by colleagues, Sen. Steve Daines, R-Mont., and Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: VA nurses are in short supply. Unions say Trump's deferred resignation plan could make things worse
item.description: Nurses caring for military veterans through the Department of Veterans Affairs comprise the largest group of federal employees.
item.dc:creator: Carla K. Johnson And Brian Witte, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship order is put on hold by a second federal judge
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered a second nationwide pause on President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for anyone born in the U.S. to someone in the country illegally, calling citizenship a “most precious right.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Black church in DC that was vandalized by the Proud Boys gains control over the group's trademark
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A judge has awarded a historic Black church in Washington control over the Proud Boys trademark after the far-right group defaulted on a $2.8 million judgment. The Monday ruling in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, NewsNation

item.title: White House defends Trump's Gaza takeover, resettlement proposal
item.description: The White House on Wednesday defended President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take control of the Gaza Strip amid blowback from Arab leaders and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Racial gap widened in deaths among US moms around the time of childbirth
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Black women in the U.S. died at a rate nearly 3.5 times higher white women around the time of childbirth in 2023, as maternal mortality fell below prepandemic levels overall but racial gaps widened, according to federal health data released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeremiah Trotter Jr. continues legacy of Eagles Super Bowl linebackers
item.description: His dad, known as the "Ax Man" for his signature sack celebration, played eight seasons in Midnight Green and is a member of the team's Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Rare invasive bug intercepted at Detroit airport
item.description: Agriculture specialists with U.S. Customs and Border Protection confirm that rare invasive seed bugs were intercepted on undeclared cargo late last year at Detroit Metro Airport.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KRON4

item.title: Texas man set to be executed for killing a pastor in his own church
item.description: A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his own church during a robbery was facing execution on Wednesday, the second scheduled execution in the U.S. so far this year. Steven Lawayne Nelson was condemned for the 2011 killing of the Rev. Clint Dobson.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI agents who 'simply followed orders' in Jan. 6 probes won't be fired, a Justice official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI agents “who simply followed orders and carried out their duties in an ethical manner” while investigating the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In bid for Davos-like gathering, Semafor plans to bring at least 200 CEOs to Washington conference
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The media company Semafor is planning a Washington economic conference for global CEOs this April that it hopes will rival the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, for influence.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill would allow schools to opt out of enrolling undocumented students
item.description: Senate Democratic leadership responded to the bill Tuesday, calling it "unconstitutional" and "prejudiced.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: Mahomes reveals thoughts on Trump attending the Super Bowl
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes was asked what it will feel like to play in front of a sitting president.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, Fox 5

item.title: Racial gap widened in deaths among US moms around the time of childbirth
item.description: Black women in the U.S. died at a rate nearly 3.5 times higher white women around the time of childbirth in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs have led military and government agencies — along with some private companies — to roll back observances of diversity and celebrations of Black History Month, which goes through February.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he wants US to take charge of Gaza Strip at press conference with Netanyahu
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip and redevelop it after Palestinians are resettled elsewhere. He made the remarks in a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Oregon Zoo welcomes a new baby elephant
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — The Oregon Zoo in Portland has welcomed its newest addition, a baby elephant. Thirty-year-old Asian elephant Rose-Tu gave birth on Saturday after 20 months of pregnancy, the zoo said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Musk adds Shell, Nestlé, Lego to X advertiser 'boycott' lawsuit
item.description: X is adding several more companies to its lawsuit accusing firms of conspiring on an advertising "boycott" on the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Officials say remains of 55 of 67 victims of midair collision have been recovered and identified
item.description: Families of victims of the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001 visited the crash site on Sunday and divers scoured the submerged wreckage for more remains after authorities said they've recovered and identified 55 of the 67 people killed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doncic releases emotional farewell message to Dallas after trade sends him to Lakers
item.description: Luka Doncic released an emotional farewell to Dallas on Sunday, the same day a trade sending him from the Mavericks to the Los Angeles Lakers in a swap for fellow All-NBA player Anthony Davis was finalized.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black box recovered in Philadelphia medical transport jet crash: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder was located at the site of initial impact, at a depth of 8 feet, according to the NTSB.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, WGN 9

item.title: Passengers, crew killed in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia identified
item.description: Authorities said a seventh person, who had been in a car at the time of the crash, has died. Their identity has not yet been released.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4
